applicationDefinition: A conversational system that answers common library questions and guides users to services and resources.
libraryExample: A public library chatbot answers questions about hours, borrowing policies, and upcoming programs.

applicationDefinition: An AI tool that helps users plan and refine research by suggesting terms, sources, and search strategies.
libraryExample: A university library research assistant helps a student build search terms for a literature review.

applicationDefinition: A tool that helps users create, format, or check citations in a chosen citation style.
libraryExample: A student pastes in source information and gets help converting citations into APA format.

applicationDefinition: A system that produces shorter versions of long articles, reports, or books while preserving key points.
libraryExample: A graduate student uses summaries to decide which journal articles to read in full.

applicationDefinition: A language-support tool that helps staff and patrons communicate across languages.
libraryExample: A librarian uses translation support to assist a patron who prefers Spanish.

applicationDefinition: A search approach that retrieves results by meaning and intent instead of exact keyword matches.
libraryExample: A search for climate impacts on farming returns items about drought, agriculture, and food security.

applicationDefinition: A recommendation system that suggests resources based on user behavior, interests, or usage patterns.
libraryExample: A reader who borrows mystery novels gets recommendations for new crime fiction titles.

applicationDefinition: A search feature that adds related terms, synonyms, or broader subjects to improve retrieval.
libraryExample: A search for 'heart attack' suggests related medical terms such as myocardial infarction.

applicationDefinition: A reading recommendation tool that suggests books or media and explains how they match a user's interests.
libraryExample: A patron asks for read-alikes to a favorite fantasy novel and receives suggested titles with brief appeal notes.

applicationDefinition: A cataloging support tool that suggests subject headings, descriptors, or keywords from item content.
libraryExample: A cataloger reviews subject heading suggestions for a newly acquired book.

applicationDefinition: A tool that creates draft descriptions, abstracts, keywords, or other metadata for review by staff.
libraryExample: A repository generates a draft description for a faculty publication before staff review it.

applicationDefinition: A quality-control tool that identifies inconsistent, incomplete, or potentially incorrect metadata records.
libraryExample: A library uses AI to flag records with missing publication dates and inconsistent subject terms.

applicationDefinition: A tool that recommends classification numbers or categories based on the content of a resource.
libraryExample: A system suggests a Dewey Decimal number for a newly acquired title.

applicationDefinition: A text-analysis tool that identifies names, places, organizations, dates, and other entities in documents.
libraryExample: An archive extracts names and locations from oral history transcripts.

applicationDefinition: A matching tool that identifies likely duplicate records so staff can merge or resolve them.
libraryExample: A shared catalog flags multiple records that appear to describe the same edition.

applicationDefinition: A digitization tool that converts scanned images of text into machine-readable text.
libraryExample: A library digitizes historical newspapers and makes them searchable by keyword.

applicationDefinition: A vision-based tool that converts handwritten text into machine-readable text.
libraryExample: An archive transcribes handwritten letters so researchers can search them digitally.

applicationDefinition: A computer vision tool that identifies objects, people, places, or themes in images.
libraryExample: A photograph collection is automatically tagged with subject terms and locations.

applicationDefinition: A speech technology tool that converts recorded audio or video into written text.
libraryExample: A library creates transcripts for oral history interviews to improve access and searchability.

applicationDefinition: A generative tool that creates draft descriptions for items in digital collections or archives.
libraryExample: A digital exhibit receives draft descriptions for photographs before staff review them.

applicationDefinition: An analytics process that identifies subjects, communities, or perspectives that may be underrepresented in a collection.
libraryExample: A public library checks whether Indigenous authors are adequately represented in its holdings.

applicationDefinition: A prediction tool that estimates future demand for materials, services, or formats.
libraryExample: A library predicts increased demand for tax-preparation materials during the spring.

applicationDefinition: A decision-support tool that suggests materials for purchase based on needs, trends, or usage patterns.
libraryExample: A selector receives recommended titles in an emerging research area.

applicationDefinition: A review tool that flags materials that may deserve closer evaluation for retention, storage, or withdrawal.
libraryExample: A library generates a list of low-use items for staff review before making weeding decisions.

applicationDefinition: An analytics process that examines circulation, database use, website activity, or service metrics.
libraryExample: A library reviews database usage data before renewing a subscription.

applicationDefinition: A data-analysis approach that uses historical patterns to forecast future outcomes or needs.
libraryExample: A library predicts future study room demand based on prior usage trends.

applicationDefinition: A text-analysis technique that identifies opinions, emotions, or attitudes in written feedback.
libraryExample: A library analyzes survey comments to understand how patrons feel about a new service.

applicationDefinition: An interactive learning tool that teaches research skills, source evaluation, and information literacy concepts.
libraryExample: A student gets guided practice evaluating whether a source is credible.

applicationDefinition: A tool that rewrites content in simpler language to improve understanding and accessibility.
libraryExample: A library provides plain-language summaries of government documents for community members.

applicationDefinition: A translation tool that converts text or speech between languages to support communication and access.
libraryExample: A patron uses a service desk kiosk that translates catalog instructions into their preferred language.

applicationDefinition: A productivity tool that creates summaries, action items, and notes from meetings or discussions.
libraryExample: A library committee receives an automatically generated summary after a planning meeting.

applicationDefinition: A writing assistant that helps staff draft internal policies, procedures, or guidelines.
libraryExample: Staff draft a circulation policy outline before editing it for local needs.

applicationDefinition: A tool that drafts routine reports by assembling and summarizing information from multiple sources.
libraryExample: A library generates a draft annual report from assessment data and service metrics.

applicationDefinition: A writing assistant that helps draft, refine, or organize grant proposals.
libraryExample: A researcher uses AI to outline a grant narrative and strengthen the language in a proposal.

applicationDefinition: An analysis tool that visualizes themes, connections, or trends across a body of research.
libraryExample: A librarian maps the main topics appearing in recent articles on digital humanities.

applicationDefinition: An analytics process that examines citations, altmetrics, or collaboration patterns to estimate research influence.
libraryExample: A library helps a faculty member review citation trends for a promotion dossier.

applicationDefinition: A tool that helps researchers draft plans for organizing, preserving, and sharing research data.
libraryExample: A graduate student uses AI to outline a data management plan for a grant application.

applicationDefinition: A curation tool that creates thematic reading lists based on topics, events, courses, or interests.
libraryExample: A librarian generates a reading list for a community discussion on climate resilience.

applicationDefinition: A natural-language search tool that lets users ask questions instead of building keyword searches.
libraryExample: A patron asks, "Which beginner gardening books cover container planting?" and receives catalog results with short explanations.

applicationDefinition: An AI system that can plan and carry out multi-step research tasks with limited human intervention.
libraryExample: A graduate student asks the system to identify major themes in recent AI-in-education literature.
